title: When Prayer Feels Like Talking to the Ceiling
scripture_text: The Spirit himself intercedes for us with sighs too deep for words.
reflection: Even when you cannot pray, the Spirit prays for you. Your silence is not emptiness; it is a space the Spirit fills.
body:
You sat down to pray. The words did not come. They rarely do, on the days you most need them.

That is okay. Sit there anyway. Let the silence be the prayer. God is not waiting for the right phrases. He is with you in the quiet.

Sometimes prayer is a long, slow exhale. Sometimes it is a walk in the woods. Sometimes it is sitting on a kitchen floor at 3am, holding a coffee mug that has gone cold.

All of it is prayer. None of it is wasted. The Spirit is doing the work your words cannot do.
prayer: Lord, I have no words. You do not need them. Sit with me. Amen.
